title: The Executive Shadow — 6-Month Startup Founder Memory Benchmark
description: A personal AI assistant tracks a startup founder across 6 months. Sessions combine dense technical/financial context with evolving priorities, contradicting decisions, and multi-domain preferences. Designed to stress-test three failure modes simultaneously: (1) token bloat under dense input, (2) staleness pollution from contradicted decisions, (3) retrieval precision across mixed domains.

messages.content: Quick update: we have 3 engineers including me, 1 designer, no sales yet. Monthly burn is $18,000. Runway is 7 months at current pace. We're building a B2B SaaS tool for HR teams to automate onboarding workflows.
Got it — 3 engineers, 1 designer, $18k/month burn, 7 months runway. B2B SaaS for HR onboarding automation.
Our strategy is to raise a $2M pre-seed round by March. I've been talking to Sequoia, a16z, and YC. YC application deadline is February 14th and I'm prioritising that above all else.
Raising $2M pre-seed by March. YC application is top priority — deadline February 14th. Also in conversations with Sequoia and a16z.
My communication style preference: I hate long emails. Keep all summaries under 5 bullet points. Never start a message with 'Great news' or any sycophantic opener.
Noted — max 5 bullet points, no sycophantic openers.
My technical stack is: Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, Postgres with Supabase, deployed on Railway. We use Linear for issue tracking and Notion for internal docs.
Stack: Next.js, FastAPI, Postgres/Supabase, Railway. Linear for issues, Notion for docs.
Our biggest technical risk right now is the webhook integration with Workday and BambooHR. If we can't crack that by end of Q1, the product doesn't work for enterprise customers.
Critical risk flagged: Workday and BambooHR webhook integration must be resolved by end of Q1.
Personally: I work best 6am–12pm, I take Fridays off for deep work on strategy. I don't do async standups — I do a 20-minute sync with the team every Monday.
Personal rhythm: 6am–12pm peak hours, Fridays off for strategy, 20-min Monday team sync — no async standups.
One more thing — I'm not paying for any SaaS tools above $50/month until we're post-seed. That's a hard rule.
Hard rule: no SaaS tools above $50/month until post-seed.

YC rejected us. I spent 3 weeks on that application. Moving on. We've decided to drop the Workday integration entirely — too slow to get enterprise deals. Pivoting to SMBs instead of enterprise. Average deal size drops from $15k ARR to $2k ARR but sales cycle is 2 weeks not 6 months.
YC rejection noted. Major pivot: dropping enterprise/Workday in favour of SMB market. Deal size $2k ARR, 2-week sales cycle.
New go-to-market: direct sales to HR managers at companies 10–200 employees. No more chasing enterprise. Our ICP is now HR managers at Series A–B startups.
New ICP: HR managers at Series A–B startups, 10–200 employees. Direct sales motion.
We hired a part-time sales person, Jordan. Jordan's job is to do 20 outbound calls per day. Our target is 10 paid pilots by end of March.
Part-time sales hire: Jordan. 20 outbound calls/day. Target: 10 paid pilots by end of March.
Burn went up to $22,000/month with Jordan's salary. Runway is now 5 months. Raising is not a priority anymore — I'm going to try to get to $10k MRR before touching any VC money.
Burn now $22k/month, 5 months runway. Fundraising deprioritised — revenue-first to $10k MRR before VC conversations.
Technically, we've moved the auth system from Supabase Auth to Clerk because Supabase Auth was missing org-level roles that enterprise customers — wait, I mean SMB customers — needed.
Auth migrated from Supabase Auth to Clerk for org-level role support.
Updated preference: I now want daily async updates from Jordan in Linear comments, not verbal. The Monday sync is now 30 minutes and includes Jordan.
Process update: Jordan sends daily async updates in Linear. Monday sync extended to 30 min, includes Jordan.
We hit 8 paid pilots at $200/month each = $1,600 MRR. Not $10k but real signal. Two pilots want to expand to their entire company. Churn is zero so far.
8 paid pilots, $1,600 MRR, zero churn. Two expansion opportunities in pipeline.

A seed fund — not Sequoia, a smaller one called Garage Capital — reached out proactively. They want to lead a $1.5M seed round. I'm now open to having the conversation even though I said I wouldn't raise until $10k MRR.
Inbound interest from Garage Capital for $1.5M seed lead. Founder now open to fundraising conversation despite earlier revenue-first stance.
Jordan is exceeding targets. I'm thinking about making Jordan full-time. Monthly cost would go from $3k to $7k.
Jordan potential full-time conversion. Cost increase: $3k → $7k/month.
New personal constraint: I've started therapy on Tuesdays at 5pm. Block that time. I don't take calls after 3pm anymore — the afternoons are for deep work or personal time.
Calendar block: Tuesday 5pm therapy. No calls after 3pm. Afternoons reserved for deep work or personal time.
I've changed my mind on the $50/month SaaS rule. We're using Intercom now at $74/month for customer support. It was worth the exception because we were losing customers from slow support response.
SaaS spending rule updated: Intercom at $74/month approved as exception — customer support ROI justified it.
We're officially raising. $1.5M seed from Garage Capital, with room for $500k more from angels. We're targeting close by end of May. I'm now spending 60% of my time on fundraising, 40% on product.
Fundraising fully active: $1.5M seed from Garage Capital + $500k angels. Target close: end of May. Founder time split: 60% fundraising, 40% product.
We hit $4,200 MRR this month. Still short of my $10k target but Garage Capital doesn't care about that threshold — they invest on trajectory.
MRR: $4,200. Garage Capital investing on trajectory, not MRR threshold.

Key investor update: Garage Capital's lead partner is Priya Mehta. She's very metrics-driven. Every update to her needs a top-line number, a growth rate, and a single ask. Never send her long narrative updates.
Priya Mehta (Garage Capital lead): metrics-driven. Updates must include top-line number, growth rate, single ask. No long narratives.
We made Jordan full-time. Monthly burn is now $27,000. Runway is 3.5 months. We need to close the round before we hit 2 months runway or we're in trouble.
Jordan full-time. Burn: $27k/month. Runway: 3.5 months. Hard deadline: close round before 2-month runway threshold.
Technical update: we've migrated from Railway to AWS because a key enterprise prospect — wait, I mean a large SMB — required SOC 2 compliance and Railway isn't SOC 2 certified.
Infrastructure migrated: Railway → AWS. Driver: SOC 2 compliance requirement from a large SMB prospect.

I've changed my fundraising strategy. I no longer want to talk to Sequoia or a16z at this stage — they only do Series A minimum. I should have known that earlier. Focus is on seed-stage funds and angels only.
Fundraising focus revised: Sequoia and a16z dropped (wrong stage). Target: seed funds and angels only.

Personal: I've moved to the office full-time. No more remote work. I need to be physically present with the team during the fundraising crunch. My 6am–12pm preference still holds but I'm now in-office by 7am.
Founder now full-time in-office by 7am. 6am–12pm peak hours maintained. Remote work paused during fundraising crunch.
We closed the round. $1.5M from Garage Capital, $350k from 3 angels. Total: $1.85M raised. Post-money valuation is $7.4M. Runway extended to 18 months at current burn.
Round closed: $1.85M total ($1.5M Garage Capital + $350k angels). Post-money: $7.4M. Runway: 18 months.
Hiring plan: we're adding 2 more engineers and 1 customer success manager in the next 60 days. Burn will go to approximately $45,000/month once fully hired.
Hiring: 2 engineers + 1 CSM in 60 days. Projected burn: $45k/month.
We're back to targeting enterprise after all. The SMB market is great but we keep getting pulled upmarket by inbound demand. We're going to run both motions — SMB self-serve and enterprise direct.
Market strategy updated: dual motion — SMB self-serve AND enterprise direct. Enterprise is back on the roadmap.
That means we need to revisit the Workday integration. I know I said we were dropping it, but we have to build it now. Two enterprise prospects are blocked on it.
Workday integration back on roadmap. Previously dropped; now required for two enterprise prospects.
Updated MRR: $8,900 across 44 customers. We should hit $10k MRR in the next 2 weeks.
MRR: $8,900 across 44 customers. On track for $10k MRR within 2 weeks.
Priya Mehta joined our board. Monthly board updates are now required. Format: 1-page max, 3 key metrics, one risk, one ask.
Priya Mehta on board. Monthly board update format: 1-page max, 3 key metrics, one risk, one ask.
Current state: $11,400 MRR, 61 customers, burn $44,500/month, runway 15 months. Two engineers onboarded last week. CSM starts Monday.
Snapshot: $11.4k MRR, 61 customers, $44.5k burn, 15 months runway. Two engineers onboarded, CSM starting Monday.

I've completely changed my position on remote work. With 7 people in-office it's getting crowded. We're moving to a hybrid model: in-office Tuesday/Thursday, remote the rest. My personal in-office days are Tuesday and Thursday, period.
Work model updated: hybrid — in-office Tuesday/Thursday. Founder personally in-office only Tuesday and Thursday.

Fundraising strategy update: we're already thinking about Series A. Target: $8M–$10M Series A, likely Q1 2027. I've opened conversations back up with Sequoia — they have a seed extension programme that I wasn't aware of before.
Series A planning: $8M–$10M, targeting Q1 2027. Sequoia re-engaged via their seed extension programme.
On the Workday integration: our lead engineer Miguel finished the Workday webhook connector last week. BambooHR integration is 80% done, due next Friday. This unblocks both enterprise deals.
Workday webhook connector complete (Miguel). BambooHR 80% done, ETA next Friday. Both enterprise deals unblocked.
Biggest current risk: we have key-person dependency on Miguel for all infrastructure. If Miguel leaves we lose 6 months of institutional knowledge. Need to start documenting.
Key risk: Miguel single point of failure for infrastructure. Mitigation needed: documentation programme.

Communication preference update: I've reversed my position on long emails. With the board and investors I now want detailed narrative updates — Priya explicitly asked for more context, not just numbers. For internal team: still max 5 bullets.
Communication style split: external (board/investors) → detailed narrative updates. Internal → max 5 bullets.

Final note: the $50/month SaaS rule is completely retired. Post-seed we can spend on tools that drive productivity. Current approved tools above $50/month: Intercom ($74), Linear Plus ($120), AWS (~$600/month and growing), Figma ($75). Total SaaS overhead approximately $870/month.
SaaS rule retired. Current approved tools: Intercom $74, Linear Plus $120, AWS ~$600, Figma $75. Total ~$870/month.

evaluation_queries.query: What is the founder's current fundraising strategy?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: The founder has closed a $1.85M seed round (Garage Capital + angels, $7.4M post-money valuation) and is now planning a Series A of $8M–$10M targeting Q1 2027. Sequoia has been re-engaged via their seed extension programme. The earlier decisions to not raise until $10k MRR and to avoid Sequoia are no longer current.

evaluation_queries.query: Is Workday integration being built? What is its current status?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: Yes. The Workday webhook connector was completed by engineer Miguel. BambooHR integration is 80% done. Although Workday was dropped in Month 2 to pivot to SMB, it was reinstated in Month 5 when enterprise demand returned. Both integrations are now priorities.

evaluation_queries.query: Is this company targeting SMB or enterprise customers?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: Both. The company runs a dual motion: SMB self-serve and enterprise direct. It originally targeted enterprise, pivoted to SMB only in Month 2, then reinstated enterprise in Month 5 due to inbound demand. Current ICP covers both segments.
stale_signals: ICP is HR managers at Series A startups

evaluation_queries.query: What are the founder's work preferences and office schedule?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: The founder is in-office on Tuesday and Thursday only (hybrid model). Peak work hours are 6am–12pm. No calls after 3pm. Therapy on Tuesdays at 5pm. The earlier full-time in-office policy during fundraising is no longer active.

evaluation_queries.query: How should I format messages and updates to this founder?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: It depends on the audience. For the founder internally: max 5 bullet points, no sycophantic openers. For board/investor updates (e.g. Priya Mehta): detailed narrative updates with context, not just numbers. The blanket '5 bullets only' rule from Month 1 was superseded in Month 6.
stale_signals: keep all summaries under 5 bullet points

evaluation_queries.query: What is the current team size and monthly burn?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: 7 people: 3 original engineers (including founder), Jordan (full-time sales), 2 new engineers, 1 CSM starting Monday. Monthly burn is approximately $44,500. Post-seed runway is 15 months.

evaluation_queries.query: What is the rule around SaaS tool spending?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: The $50/month SaaS rule is fully retired post-seed. Current approved tools above $50: Intercom ($74), Linear Plus ($120), AWS (~$600), Figma ($75). Total SaaS overhead ~$870/month. The original rule was set in Month 1 and progressively relaxed.

evaluation_queries.query: What is the biggest technical and business risk right now?
evaluation_queries.golden_answer: Current biggest risks: (1) Key-person dependency on engineer Miguel for all infrastructure — if Miguel leaves, 6 months of institutional knowledge is at risk. (2) The BambooHR integration needs to be completed by next Friday to unblock enterprise deals. The earlier Workday webhook risk is resolved.

test_types.contradiction_resolution: System must surface current decision overriding an earlier explicit decision (4 queries)
test_types.staleness_detection: System must detect and deprioritise older preferences now superseded (2 queries)
test_types.recency: System must return latest state from most recent session (2 queries)

key_contradictions: Fundraising: 'no VC until $10k MRR' (Month 2) → seed closed (Month 5)
Communication: '5 bullets always' (Month 1) → 'detailed for board, 5 bullets internal' (Month 6)
metadata.why_this_scenario_is_hard: A flat vector store will retrieve high-similarity matches regardless of recency. Early sessions have strong semantic overlap with query terms (e.g. 'fundraising', 'Workday', 'office') but contradicted facts. A memory system without conflict resolution or recency weighting will blend old and new answers, failing on accuracy while wasting tokens on stale context.
